hypothesis: If a reinforcement-learning molecular generator is optimized to maximize CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while minimizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity (with a QED term), then it will produce novel, synthetically accessible molecules with equal or better ER docking scores than a QVina-guided baseline because pharmacophoric alignment preserves key binding interactions while structural dissimilarity enforces scaffold diversification without sacrificing affinity.
